text.title: feature-flags-rollout-targeting Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for feature-flags-rollout-targeting as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: A/B leakage: a user opens two tabs and sees two variants because the assignment is session-scoped, not user-scoped.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Session-scoped assignment makes analytics treat one user as two. Always key on a stable user identifier (user_id), not session token or cookie.

failure-mode.description: Global flags without user context: flag applies to all traffic or none.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Without user_id, gradual rollout is impossible. The flag becomes a deploy-time toggle, not a progressive delivery tool.

failure-mode.description: LaunchDarkly (or any SaaS flag service) outage causes your app outage because is_enabled() blocks on the SDK call.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Always configure a local in-memory cache on the SDK with a stale-while-revalidate policy. The cached evaluation runs even when the flag service is unreachable.
